title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible accessible multimodal evidence retriever with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in accessible multimodal evidence retriever and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug RAG engineering team handles case 9736 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.

steps.title: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-jjeg9q" that uses case 9167 to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval, keep Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-jjeg9q
# Competency: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval.
# Case 9167: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def evidence_rag_multimodal_accessibility_eval_jjeg9q(pages: int = 8, text_regions: int = 20, image_regions: int = 6, table_regions: int = 4, coordinates_kept: bool = True, text_alternatives: bool = True, modality_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jjeg9q = "jjeg9q-q9gejj-9167"
    assert evidence_variant_jjeg9q
    evidence_axes_jjeg9q = (
        "corpusjjeg9q",
        "queryq9gejj",
        "tenant9167",
        "indexjjeg9q9167",
        "promptq9gejj9167",
        "evaluation9167jjeg9q",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jjeg9q)) == 6
    if pages <= 0 or min(text_regions, image_regions, table_regions) < 0:
        return False, 0, "document-regions"
    if not coordinates_kept:
        return False, pages, "visual-citation"
    if not text_alternatives:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-alternative"
    if not modality_evaluated:
        return False, pages, "modality-quality"
    regions = text_regions + image_regions + table_regions
    assert regions >= pages
    return True, regions, "multimodal-evidence"
